machine.name: WellnessAnalytics
machine.description: Analytics layer that consumes PatientWellness four-level metric outputs and detects longitudinal anomaly patterns — sustained deterioration, rapid decline, and recovery. Routes clinical decisions to downstream machines: NewPatientInflow (for community-dwelling patients requiring initial assisted living evaluation) and CareTransitionWorkflow (for facility residents requiring care level changes). Produces six routing outputs covering both community and facility escalation paths.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.domain: Healthcare Analytics — Wellness Monitoring and Care Routing
metadata.eventSpace: 8D cumulative wellness severity space: [WELLNESS_ACTIVE, BELOW_OPTIMAL, ALERT_OR_WORSE, CRITICAL_LEVEL, REPEAT_CYCLE, COMMUNITY_ORIGIN, DECLINE_VELOCITY, RECOVERY_SIGNAL]
metadata.outputSpace: 8D routing output space: [INFLOW_EVALUATION, INFLOW_URGENT, TRANSITION_REVIEW, TRANSITION_ESCALATION, TRANSITION_URGENT, WELLNESS_IMPROVING, _, _]

upstream.note: PatientWellness uses one-hot output encoding. Direct one-hot transitions are HD=2, violating the Single Transition Assumption. An integration layer translates PatientWellness one-hot outputs into WellnessAnalytics cumulative vectors before writing to this machine's input region at offset 234.

downstream_community.semantics: INFLOW_EVALUATION: sustained ALERT (2+ cycles) in community → initiate standard NewPatientInflow evaluation sequence. INFLOW_URGENT: CRITICAL level in community → initiate emergency NewPatientInflow with accelerated timeline.

downstream_facility.semantics: TRANSITION_REVIEW: sustained ALERT in facility → initiate care level review (Scenario 2/3 of CareTransitionWorkflow). TRANSITION_ESCALATION: CRITICAL in facility → activate CareTransitionWorkflow emergency escalation (Sequence 5). TRANSITION_URGENT: rapid 2-level decline → priority CareTransitionWorkflow engagement.

integrationLayerMapping.description: The integration layer (care coordination system, rules engine, or AI agent) is responsible for translating PatientWellness outputs into WellnessAnalytics input vectors. It must: (1) read PatientWellness one-hot output at offset 210-213; (2) convert to cumulative encoding (D0-D3); (3) assess patient's living context for D5; (4) compute velocity by comparing current vs prior assessment level for D6; (5) track consecutive cycles at same or worse level for D4; (6) detect improvement for D7; (7) write composite vector to WellnessAnalytics input at offset 234.

mappingTable.PatientWellness_OPTIMAL_community: D0=H, D1=L, D2=L, D3=L, D4=context, D5=H, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,L,L,L,?,H,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_GOOD_community: D0=H, D1=H, D2=L, D3=L, D4=context, D5=H, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,H,L,L,?,H,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_ALERT_community: D0=H, D1=H, D2=H, D3=L, D4=context, D5=H, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,H,H,L,?,H,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_CRITICAL_community: D0=H, D1=H, D2=H, D3=H, D4=context, D5=H, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,H,H,H,?,H,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_OPTIMAL_facility: D0=H, D1=L, D2=L, D3=L, D4=context, D5=L, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,L,L,L,?,L,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_GOOD_facility: D0=H, D1=H, D2=L, D3=L, D4=context, D5=L, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,H,L,L,?,L,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_ALERT_facility: D0=H, D1=H, D2=H, D3=L, D4=context, D5=L, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,H,H,L,?,L,?,?]
mappingTable.PatientWellness_CRITICAL_facility: D0=H, D1=H, D2=H, D3=H, D4=context, D5=L, D6=velocity, D7=recovery → [H,H,H,H,?,L,?,?]

contextFlagRules.D4_REPEAT_CYCLE: Set H if this is the 2nd or later consecutive assessment at ALERT or CRITICAL level for this patient. Reset to L when level improves above ALERT.
contextFlagRules.D6_DECLINE_VELOCITY: Set H if the wellness level dropped by 2 or more levels in a single assessment cycle (e.g., GOOD→CRITICAL skipping ALERT, or OPTIMAL→ALERT skipping GOOD). Reset to L at next assessment.
contextFlagRules.D7_RECOVERY_SIGNAL: Set H if current level is strictly better than the prior assessment level. Set L otherwise.

dimensions.D0_WELLNESS_ACTIVE: A completed PatientWellness assessment result is available and being processed. Always HIGH for any wellness event entering this machine. The cumulative encoding (D0=H for all levels) ensures D0 never changes within a care monitoring sequence — it is the anchor dimension.
dimensions.D1_BELOW_OPTIMAL: Patient's wellness level is GOOD, ALERT, or CRITICAL (not OPTIMAL). HIGH when PatientWellness output is GOOD, ALERT, or CRITICAL. Part of the cumulative ladder: D1=H means the resident has at least one wellness dimension out of norm.
dimensions.D2_ALERT_OR_WORSE: Patient's wellness level is ALERT or CRITICAL. HIGH when PatientWellness output is ALERT or CRITICAL. Crosses the actionable clinical threshold — at ALERT, care team review is required. D2 rising is the primary trigger for intervention sequences.
dimensions.D3_CRITICAL_LEVEL: Patient's wellness level is CRITICAL. HIGH only when PatientWellness output is CRITICAL. Mental acuity has crossed below age-cohort norm with an established decline trend. Triggers emergency routing to hospital or urgent care level escalation.
dimensions.D4_REPEAT_CYCLE: This assessment cycle is the 2nd or later consecutive reading at ALERT or CRITICAL level. Set by the integration layer when a patient has remained at or below ALERT across consecutive bi-weekly assessment periods. Distinguishes a single anomalous reading from a sustained deterioration pattern requiring structural intervention.
dimensions.D5_COMMUNITY_ORIGIN: Patient is currently living in a non-assisted community or independent living setting (home, independent living apartment, family residence). HIGH routes to NewPatientInflow for ALF evaluation. LOW (patient already in an ALF/SNF/rehab facility) routes to CareTransitionWorkflow for care level change.
dimensions.D6_DECLINE_VELOCITY: Wellness dropped two or more levels in a single assessment cycle — rapid decline detected. Examples: OPTIMAL→ALERT, GOOD→CRITICAL, OPTIMAL→CRITICAL. The velocity flag enables early intervention before the sustained-pattern sequences would fire, acknowledging that a two-level drop is an immediate clinical signal regardless of prior trajectory.
dimensions.D7_RECOVERY_SIGNAL: Current assessment level is better than the prior cycle's level — improvement detected. Used in the WELLNESS_IMPROVING sequence to confirm that a patient who was at ALERT is now at GOOD or OPTIMAL, validating that the recovery is not a noise artifact but an observed sequential improvement.

namedInputVectors.COMMUNITY_GOOD: [H,H,L,L,L,H,L,L] — GOOD level, community-dwelling resident; D5=H identifies this as a community patient
namedInputVectors.FACILITY_GOOD: [H,H,L,L,L,L,L,L] — GOOD level, resident in ALF/SNF/rehab facility
namedInputVectors.FACILITY_ALERT_REPEAT: [H,H,H,L,H,L,L,L] — ALERT level, 2nd+ consecutive cycle, facility resident
namedInputVectors.FACILITY_VELOCITY: [H,H,L,L,L,L,H,L] — below-optimal with velocity flag; rapid decline first detected before alert level confirmed
namedInputVectors.FACILITY_ALERT_RAPID: [H,H,H,L,L,L,H,L] — ALERT level confirmed with velocity flag active; rapid decline reaching alert threshold
namedInputVectors.FACILITY_STABILIZING: [H,H,L,L,L,L,L,L] — GOOD level observed after prior ALERT cycle; vector is identical to FACILITY_GOOD; sequence context (prior ALERT state) distinguishes stabilization from baseline good wellness

metadata.encodingNote: Cumulative encoding resolves the HD=2 one-hot transition problem. PatientWellness uses one-hot outputs (OPTIMAL/GOOD/ALERT/CRITICAL are mutually exclusive HIGH bits). Direct chaining would produce HD=2 transitions (one bit drops, another rises). WellnessAnalytics uses a cumulative ladder: each worse level adds one HIGH bit (D1 for GOOD, D2 for ALERT, D3 for CRITICAL), ensuring HD=1 transitions between adjacent levels and enabling monotonic severity reasoning.

vectorEncoding.semantics: GTE binary: value >= threshold → HIGH. All within-sequence transitions are HD=1. Cumulative bits D0-D3 represent severity ladder (all accumulate to H as severity worsens). D4-D7 are orthogonal context/modifier flags that fire independently.

singleTransitionAssumption.notes: All 9 within-sequence transitions verified HD=1. Sequences 3 and 5 share the FACILITY_GOOD initial; they diverge at step 2 with D2 (ALERT level, Seq 3) vs D6 (velocity flag, Seq 5). Sequences 4 and 6 share the FACILITY_ALERT initial; they diverge at step 2 with D3 (CRITICAL escalation, Seq 4) vs D2 de-asserting (recovery, Seq 6). FACILITY_STABILIZING and FACILITY_GOOD share the same vector representation; CES node identity distinguishes them within their respective sequences.

routingLogic.community_path: D5=H selects NewPatientInflow as the downstream machine. INFLOW_EVALUATION fires when sustained ALERT indicates the patient's functional/wellness decline has progressed to the point where independent living is no longer safely sustainable — the natural trigger for ALF evaluation. INFLOW_URGENT fires when CRITICAL indicates immediate clinical danger — triggers accelerated intake or emergency placement.
routingLogic.facility_path: D5=L selects CareTransitionWorkflow as the downstream machine. TRANSITION_REVIEW fires for sustained ALERT — the patient may need a higher care level (ALF→SNF, SNF→hospital observation). TRANSITION_ESCALATION fires for CRITICAL — corresponds to CareTransitionWorkflow Sequence 5 (emergency re-escalation from facility to hospital). TRANSITION_URGENT fires for rapid velocity decline — immediate care level review before the sustained-pattern sequence would fire.
routingLogic.recovery_path: WELLNESS_IMPROVING fires when a facility patient returns to GOOD level after an ALERT period. No downstream machine activation — this is a positive outcome signal for the care team dashboard indicating that prior interventions were effective. The care team should confirm improvement holds over next 2 cycles before closing the ALERT episode.

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning WellnessAnalytics matures.

rules.description: INFLOW_EVALUATION — sustained ALERT in community-dwelling patient; initiate NewPatientInflow standard evaluation sequence
INFLOW_URGENT — CRITICAL wellness in community-dwelling patient; initiate urgent NewPatientInflow evaluation with accelerated timeline
TRANSITION_REVIEW — sustained ALERT in facility resident; activate CareTransitionWorkflow for care level review
TRANSITION_ESCALATION — CRITICAL wellness in facility resident; activate CareTransitionWorkflow emergency escalation to hospital
TRANSITION_URGENT — rapid 2-level wellness decline in facility resident; initiate priority care level review without waiting for two-cycle confirmation
WELLNESS_IMPROVING — facility resident returning to GOOD level after ALERT; no care escalation required; care team notified of recovery

sequences.name: Sustained ALERT — Community Resident → NewPatientInflow Evaluation

metadata.description: A community-dwelling patient has been at ALERT wellness level for two or more consecutive bi-weekly assessment cycles. Sustained multi-dimension wellness deficit while living independently — the clinical signal that assisted living evaluation is appropriate. Routes to NewPatientInflow for standard intake evaluation.
metadata.clinicalRationale: A single ALERT reading may reflect an acute stressor (bereavement, illness, social disruption). Two consecutive readings confirm a structural pattern — the patient's independent living environment is no longer meeting their care needs. NewPatientInflow evaluation is initiated while the patient is still in a planned, non-emergency transition window.
metadata.downstreamSequence: npi-acceptance or rejection paths based on evaluation outcome

metadata.name: Community Good Wellness (Baseline)
metadata.description: Community-dwelling patient at GOOD wellness level. D5=H identifies community origin. D1=H (BELOW_OPTIMAL) and D2=L (not yet at alert). Kleene-plus: multiple consecutive GOOD readings during routine monitoring before first ALERT appears.

metadata.name: Community First ALERT
metadata.description: First ALERT-level reading for this community-dwelling patient. D2=H (ALERT_OR_WORSE) rises. HD=1 from COMMUNITY_GOOD (D2: L→H). D4=L (first occurrence, not yet repeat). Care team notified; single reading triggers monitoring increase but not yet ALF referral.

metadata.name: Community ALERT — Second Consecutive Cycle
metadata.description: Second (or later) consecutive ALERT-level reading for this community patient. D4=H (REPEAT_CYCLE). HD=1 from COMMUNITY_ALERT (D4: L→H). Sustained pattern confirmed — structural intervention warranted. Terminal node — fires INFLOW_EVALUATION output.
INFLOW_EVALUATION — sustained ALERT in community-dwelling patient; initiate NewPatientInflow standard evaluation sequence
metadata.action: Initiate NewPatientInflow evaluation. Schedule intake appointment. Inform family of evaluation recommendation. Begin prescreening checklist. Patient retains full autonomy — evaluation is a recommendation, not a mandate.
metadata.typicalTimeline: Schedule intake appointment within 10-14 days. Patient continues in community living during evaluation process.

sequences.name: CRITICAL Level — Community Resident → Urgent NewPatientInflow

metadata.description: A community-dwelling patient has reached CRITICAL wellness level — mental acuity below age-cohort norm with confirmed longitudinal decline trend. CRITICAL in a community resident indicates immediate risk: the patient cannot safely manage independent living and requires urgent placement evaluation, potentially concurrent with emergency clinical evaluation.
metadata.clinicalRationale: CRITICAL in a community resident signals cognitive decline crossing the clinical threshold while living alone or with family who may not be equipped to provide required care. Urgent ALF placement evaluation is initiated concurrently with clinical evaluation. Emergency hospital admission may be required first (via CareTransitionWorkflow Scenario 1) if the CRITICAL level is accompanied by a life-threatening condition.

metadata.name: Community ALERT (Pre-Critical Baseline)
metadata.description: Community patient at ALERT level — the typical pre-critical observation period. Kleene-plus: may have been at ALERT for multiple cycles (captured as prior INFLOW_EVALUATION output if 2+ cycles preceded). This initial state also activates concurrently with Sequence 1 — the machine begins monitoring for CRITICAL escalation from the first ALERT observation.

metadata.name: Community CRITICAL Level
metadata.description: CRITICAL wellness level in community-dwelling patient. D3=H (CRITICAL_LEVEL). HD=1 from COMMUNITY_ALERT (D3: L→H). Terminal node — fires INFLOW_URGENT. If concurrently a life-threatening clinical condition exists (falls, cardiac event), CareTransitionWorkflow Sequence 1 (acute emergency transfer) takes priority; INFLOW_URGENT is queued for post-stabilization.
INFLOW_URGENT — CRITICAL wellness in community-dwelling patient; initiate urgent NewPatientInflow evaluation with accelerated timeline
metadata.action: Initiate urgent NewPatientInflow evaluation. Notify family immediately. Physician or NP assessment within 24 hours to assess for concurrent clinical emergency. If life-threatening condition present: activate CareTransitionWorkflow Scenario 1 first, then INFLOW_URGENT upon stabilization.
metadata.typicalTimeline: Expedited intake: 48-72 hours. If patient is cognitively impaired, healthcare proxy engagement is the first priority.

sequences.name: Sustained ALERT — Facility Resident → Care Transition Review

metadata.description: An ALF or SNF resident has been at ALERT wellness level for two or more consecutive assessment cycles. Sustained deterioration within the current care level setting suggests the facility may no longer be meeting the patient's care needs. Routes to CareTransitionWorkflow for care level review.
metadata.clinicalRationale: Two consecutive ALERT cycles in a facility resident indicates the current care environment is not addressing the identified wellness deficits. The care team should convene a multidisciplinary conference and assess whether a higher level of care (ALF→SNF, SNF→hospital observation) is needed, or whether in-place care plan modifications can address the deficit.
metadata.downstreamSequences: transition-hospital-to-rehab (if SNF level needed) or transition-rehab-to-alf (if ALF to higher SNF)

metadata.name: Facility Good Wellness (Baseline)
metadata.description: Facility resident at GOOD wellness level. D5=L identifies facility origin. Kleene-plus: multiple consecutive GOOD readings before first ALERT. This initial vector is shared with Sequence 5 (rapid decline) — both sequences activate simultaneously on FACILITY_GOOD events and diverge at step 2.

metadata.name: Facility First ALERT
metadata.description: First ALERT-level reading for this facility resident. D2=H (ALERT_OR_WORSE). HD=1 from FACILITY_GOOD (D2: L→H). D4=L (first occurrence). The sequence advances to monitor for a repeat cycle. Concurrently, Sequence 4 activates monitoring for CRITICAL escalation from this state.

metadata.name: Facility ALERT — Second Consecutive Cycle
metadata.description: Second or later consecutive ALERT reading for this facility resident. D4=H (REPEAT_CYCLE). HD=1 from FACILITY_ALERT (D4: L→H). Sustained pattern confirmed — care level review warranted. Terminal — fires TRANSITION_REVIEW.
TRANSITION_REVIEW — sustained ALERT in facility resident; activate CareTransitionWorkflow for care level review
metadata.action: Convene interdisciplinary team meeting within 5 days. Assess whether current facility level of care can address identified wellness deficits with care plan modification, or whether a higher level of care (SNF, memory care, hospital) is clinically indicated. Initiate CareTransitionWorkflow planned transition sequences if care level change is warranted.

sequences.name: CRITICAL Level — Facility Resident → Emergency Care Transition

metadata.description: An ALF or SNF resident has reached CRITICAL wellness level — mental acuity below age-cohort norm with confirmed longitudinal decline trend. CRITICAL in a facility resident indicates the current care environment is not sufficient and emergency care transition is required. Routes to CareTransitionWorkflow emergency escalation (Sequence 5).
metadata.clinicalRationale: CRITICAL in a facility resident signals that the existing care level has been insufficient to halt cognitive or multi-system decline. The patient requires a higher level of clinical intervention than the current facility can provide. CareTransitionWorkflow Sequence 5 (emergency re-escalation) is activated: the patient is transferred to hospital for evaluation, stabilization, and a comprehensive care plan revision.

metadata.name: Facility ALERT (Pre-Critical Baseline)
metadata.description: Facility resident at ALERT level — the pre-critical monitoring state. Kleene-plus: prior alert cycles may have already produced a TRANSITION_REVIEW output (Sequence 3). This initial state activates concurrently with Sequence 3 and Sequence 6, monitoring for CRITICAL escalation or recovery from the same ALERT state.

metadata.name: Facility CRITICAL Level
metadata.description: CRITICAL wellness level in facility resident. D3=H (CRITICAL_LEVEL). HD=1 from FACILITY_ALERT (D3: L→H). Terminal — fires TRANSITION_ESCALATION. Physician notification is immediate. CareTransitionWorkflow emergency escalation sequence activated.
TRANSITION_ESCALATION — CRITICAL wellness in facility resident; activate CareTransitionWorkflow emergency escalation to hospital
metadata.action: Notify attending physician immediately. Activate CareTransitionWorkflow Sequence 5 (emergency escalation). Assess for reversible CRITICAL causes (UTI, dehydration, medication toxicity, acute depression) before activating hospital transfer — if reversible cause identified and treatable in facility, physician may elect to treat in place and re-assess. Family notification mandatory within 24 hours.

sequences.name: Rapid Decline — Facility Resident → Urgent Care Transition

metadata.description: A facility resident's wellness level drops two or more steps in a single assessment cycle — a velocity-based anomaly that bypasses the sustained-pattern requirement. The rapid decline signal fires an urgent care transition review before the two-cycle confirmation period that Sequence 3 requires, acknowledging that a sudden large drop is an immediate clinical signal.
metadata.clinicalRationale: A two-level wellness drop in one bi-weekly cycle (e.g., OPTIMAL→ALERT or GOOD→CRITICAL) suggests a new acute condition, a significant environmental disruption, or a medication change with adverse effects. This cannot wait for the two-cycle confirmation protocol — the velocity itself is the anomaly that demands immediate investigation.
metadata.downstreamSequence: transition-blocked (if barrier exists) or planned transition sequences based on clinical assessment
metadata.divergenceNote: Sequences 3 and 5 share the FACILITY_GOOD initial. They diverge at step 2: Sequence 3 observes FACILITY_ALERT (D2↑, alert level rises), while Sequence 5 observes FACILITY_VELOCITY (D6↑, velocity flag rises with D2 still L). The CES evaluates both paths simultaneously and advances the matching sequence when the next event disambiguates.

metadata.name: Facility Good Wellness (Pre-Velocity Baseline)
metadata.description: Facility resident at GOOD level — the baseline state before rapid decline is detected. Shared initial vector with Sequence 3. Kleene-plus allows multiple prior GOOD readings. The sequence advances when D6 (velocity) rises, distinguishing this path from Sequence 3 which advances when D2 (alert level) rises.

metadata.name: Velocity Flag Raised
metadata.description: The integration layer has set D6=H indicating that this assessment cycle's wellness score dropped ≥2 levels from the prior cycle. D2 is still L — the full alert level classification is pending confirmation at the next node. HD=1 from FACILITY_GOOD (D6: L→H). This intermediate state ensures the rapid-decline path is HD=1 compliant by separating the velocity detection from the alert classification.

metadata.name: Rapid Decline — Alert Level Confirmed with Velocity
metadata.description: ALERT or worse level confirmed (D2=H) with velocity flag still active (D6=H). HD=1 from FACILITY_VELOCITY (D2: L→H). The combination of D2=H and D6=H uniquely identifies a velocity-driven alert — distinct from a normal gradual alert (D2=H, D6=L). Terminal — fires TRANSITION_URGENT.
TRANSITION_URGENT — rapid 2-level wellness decline in facility resident; initiate priority care level review without waiting for two-cycle confirmation
metadata.action: Physician or NP assessment within 24 hours. Investigate cause of rapid decline: new diagnosis, medication change, acute illness, environmental disruption, significant loss event. If clinical cause identified and reversible in current setting: treat in place and re-assess at next cycle. If cause requires higher level of care: activate CareTransitionWorkflow planned or emergency transition. Do not wait for Sequence 3 two-cycle confirmation — TRANSITION_URGENT is the velocity-triggered parallel path.
metadata.urgencyOverride: TRANSITION_URGENT overrides the two-cycle waiting period of TRANSITION_REVIEW. Both may fire within the same monitoring period if the rapid decline is sustained.

sequences.name: Wellness Recovery — Facility Resident Returning to GOOD Level

metadata.description: A facility resident who has been at ALERT level returns to GOOD wellness — improvement detected. No downstream machine activation. This is a positive outcome signal indicating that prior care plan interventions were effective. The care team is notified to continue monitoring for two more cycles to confirm the recovery is sustained before closing the ALERT episode.
metadata.clinicalRationale: Returning to GOOD after ALERT indicates that targeted interventions (counseling, medication adjustment, physical therapy engagement, social programming) have addressed the identified deficits. The WELLNESS_IMPROVING signal allows the care team dashboard to close the active alert episode and confirm the patient is no longer on a deterioration trajectory — no escalation to NewPatientInflow or CareTransitionWorkflow is needed.
metadata.vectorNote: FACILITY_STABILIZING and FACILITY_GOOD share the same raw vector [H,H,L,L,L,L,L,L]. CES node identity within Sequence 6 correctly routes to WELLNESS_IMPROVING rather than treating this as a new baseline GOOD event. The distinction is sequentially meaningful: observing GOOD after ALERT is qualitatively different from observing GOOD in steady-state monitoring.
metadata.divergenceNote: Sequences 4 and 6 share the FACILITY_ALERT initial. They diverge at step 2: Sequence 4 observes FACILITY_CRITICAL (D3↑, worsening), while Sequence 6 observes FACILITY_STABILIZING (D2↓, improving). The CES evaluates both simultaneously and advances the matching path.

metadata.name: Facility ALERT (Recovery Baseline)
metadata.description: Facility resident at ALERT level — the state from which recovery will be observed. Kleene-plus: the patient may have been at ALERT for multiple cycles (triggering TRANSITION_REVIEW from Sequence 3) before improvement is detected. This sequence activates simultaneously with Sequences 3 and 4 on FACILITY_ALERT events.

metadata.name: Stabilizing — Returned to GOOD Level
metadata.description: Patient's wellness level has improved from ALERT to GOOD. D2=L (ALERT_OR_WORSE de-asserted). HD=1 from FACILITY_ALERT (D2: H→L). Terminal — fires WELLNESS_IMPROVING. The raw vector [H,H,L,L,L,L,L,L] is identical to FACILITY_GOOD; however, in this sequence context (preceded by FACILITY_ALERT), it semantically represents improvement rather than sustained baseline wellness.
WELLNESS_IMPROVING — facility resident returning to GOOD level after ALERT; no care escalation required; care team notified of recovery
metadata.action: Notify care team of wellness improvement. Do NOT cancel any pending TRANSITION_REVIEW meetings — confirm recovery holds at next assessment cycle before closing the ALERT episode. Document which interventions correlated with the improvement. Maintain increased monitoring frequency for 2 more cycles. If recovery is confirmed at next cycle, de-escalate to standard bi-weekly monitoring.

inputSequences.name: TC-01: Community Resident — Sustained ALERT → NewPatientInflow Evaluation
inputSequences.description: INFLOW_EVALUATION: 76-year-old female living alone in independent apartment. Two consecutive bi-weekly wellness assessments at GOOD (stress elevated post-bereavement). Third assessment: ALERT (anxiety elevated, exercise stopped after minor fall, nutrition declining — MNA dropped below threshold). Fourth assessment: ALERT again (D4=H, repeat cycle). Second consecutive ALERT triggers NewPatientInflow evaluation recommendation. Family notified; intake appointment scheduled.

metadata.patientWellnessSource: PatientWellness output sequence: GOOD→GOOD→ALERT→ALERT at offset 210-211→210-211→210-212→210-212

inputSequences.name: TC-02: Community Resident — CRITICAL Level → Urgent NewPatientInflow
inputSequences.description: INFLOW_URGENT: 81-year-old male living with adult son. ALERT for two prior cycles (Sequence 1 already fired INFLOW_EVALUATION). Before intake appointment is scheduled, third assessment confirms CRITICAL — MoCA drops from 19 to 14 (below 85+ threshold of 18), active decline trend. Son reports increasing confusion, not recognizing neighbors, found wandering twice. INFLOW_URGENT overrides the standard evaluation timeline; 48-hour expedited intake initiated.

metadata.downstreamActivation: NewPatientInflow urgent evaluation; concurrent physician assessment for reversible causes

inputSequences.name: TC-03: Facility Resident — Sustained ALERT → CareTransitionWorkflow Review
inputSequences.description: TRANSITION_REVIEW: 78-year-old female in assisted living for 8 months. Two prior GOOD cycles. Assessment cycle 7: ALERT — anxiety, stress, and exercise all below norm (new atrial fibrillation diagnosis creating health anxiety; fall fear limiting activity). Assessment cycle 9: ALERT again (D4=H). No improvement despite care plan adjustment. CareTransitionWorkflow care level review initiated; interdisciplinary team conference scheduled.

metadata.downstreamActivation: CareTransitionWorkflow care conference; possible transition to SNF if memory-care level needed

inputSequences.name: TC-04: Facility Resident — CRITICAL Level → Emergency Care Transition
inputSequences.description: TRANSITION_ESCALATION: 86-year-old male in ALF with two prior ALERT cycles (TRANSITION_REVIEW fired at cycle 2, care conference held). Assessment cycle 5: CRITICAL — MMSE dropped from 20 to 14, active decline trend (D6 in PatientWellness set 3 cycles ago). Cognitive decline has crossed the age-cohort threshold despite in-facility interventions. CareTransitionWorkflow Sequence 5 activated: physician assessment for reversible causes; if none, emergency hospital transfer for comprehensive neuropsychological evaluation.

metadata.downstreamActivation: CareTransitionWorkflow transition-emergency-escalation; physician notified; possible hospital transfer

inputSequences.name: TC-05: Facility Resident — Rapid 2-Level Decline → Urgent Transition
inputSequences.description: TRANSITION_URGENT: 74-year-old female in ALF, previously at GOOD level for 4 consecutive cycles. New assessment: PatientWellness reports ALERT (3 dims below norm) AND the integration layer sets D6=H — the prior cycle was OPTIMAL, making this a 2-level drop (OPTIMAL→ALERT) in one bi-weekly period. Correlation: new medication started 3 weeks ago. TRANSITION_URGENT fires without waiting for two-cycle confirmation; physician assessment within 24 hours, medication review initiated.

metadata.patientWellnessSource: PatientWellness output: GOOD→GOOD (D5/OPTIMAL prior cycle), integration layer sets D6=H on third event (velocity: OPTIMAL→ALERT in one cycle)
metadata.downstreamActivation: CareTransitionWorkflow priority review; medication reconciliation; possible transition if cause not reversible
metadata.velocityContext: Prior cycle: PatientWellness OPTIMAL. Current cycle: PatientWellness ALERT. Delta = 3 levels (OPTIMAL=4, ALERT=2) → velocity flag D6=H

inputSequences.name: TC-06: Facility Resident — ALERT Then Recovery → Wellness Improving
inputSequences.description: WELLNESS_IMPROVING: 79-year-old female in ALF. ALERT for two cycles (TRANSITION_REVIEW fired; care conference held; interventions implemented: anxiety counseling started, walking group enrollment, nutrition supplement added). Third assessment: GOOD level restored across 5 of 6 dimensions. D2=L (ALERT_OR_WORSE de-asserted). WELLNESS_IMPROVING fires. Care team documents effective intervention. TRANSITION_REVIEW episode closed after two confirming cycles.

metadata.downstreamActivation: None — recovery signal; no escalation required
metadata.interventionCorrelation: Anxiety counseling + exercise engagement + nutritional supplement implemented after TRANSITION_REVIEW care conference at cycle 1. Recovery confirmed at cycle 3.

inputSequences.name: TC-07: Community Resident — Multiple GOOD Cycles (Kleene+) Before ALERT → INFLOW_EVALUATION
inputSequences.description: INFLOW_EVALUATION: 68-year-old male living independently. Three consecutive GOOD wellness cycles during routine bi-monthly community wellness checks. Fourth assessment: ALERT. Fifth assessment: ALERT repeat. Validates Kleene-plus COMMUNITY_GOOD initial semantics — three prior GOOD readings are consumed before the sequence advances on ALERT. INFLOW_EVALUATION fires on the second consecutive ALERT.

metadata.kleenePlusNote: Three identical COMMUNITY_GOOD vectors consumed by Kleene-plus initial before sequence advances.

inputSequences.name: TC-08: Facility Resident — Multiple ALERT Cycles (Kleene+) Then CRITICAL → TRANSITION_ESCALATION
inputSequences.description: TRANSITION_ESCALATION: 88-year-old female in memory-care ALF unit. ALERT for three consecutive assessment cycles — TRANSITION_REVIEW (Sequence 3) fired at cycle 2. Care conference held; additional cognitive support added; family meeting conducted. Cycle 4 assessment: CRITICAL — cognitive decline has crossed clinical threshold despite all in-facility interventions. Three prior ALERT cycles consumed by Kleene-plus initial of Sequence 4 before CRITICAL fires TRANSITION_ESCALATION. Validates Kleene-plus semantics on FACILITY_ALERT initial.

metadata.kleenePlusNote: Three identical FACILITY_ALERT vectors consumed by Kleene-plus initial before FACILITY_CRITICAL advances to output.
metadata.sequenceInterplay: Sequences 3 (TRANSITION_REVIEW at cycle 2) and 4 (TRANSITION_ESCALATION at cycle 4) fired from the same monitoring arc — Sequence 3 provides early warning, Sequence 4 escalates when in-facility interventions prove insufficient.
